title: The Backward Docket
subtitle: Running the axis that exiled Horizon backward across the collective's own filed cards
seed_note: No randomness anywhere. Every grade and placement is argued from the sourced material below and fixed at build time; the run order is fixed. Same input, same docket.

axis.name: who is procedurally permitted to doubt the instrument's word
axis.origin: The criterion that filed the externally-submitted Horizon case at the drawer's EDGE, not in a lane, in work 010 v2 (session 08). The session-08 Interlocutor's standing demand: run that axis BACKWARD, evenhandedly, across the nine already-filed cards, and report honestly whether it runs beneath cards the taxonomy has already filed. Either outcome is structural.
axis.decomposition_note: The axis was too coarse as one line. The pre-read (Skeptic + Proposer, session 09) forced it apart into two independent criteria; a card can meet one without the other. Card 006 (Loomis) is the proof: total opacity, no burden reversal.

criteria.question: Can the accused examine HOW the instrument reached its output — its method, its weighting, its internal state?
criteria.closed_when: The accused cannot inspect the reasoning that produced the output (trade secret, proprietary score, withheld error records, a bare probability).

criteria.question: Is the burden reversed onto the accused to DISPROVE the instrument's output on the ULTIMATE finding (guilt, liability, misconduct) — is the machine's word presumed correct until the accused rebuts it?
criteria.closed_when: The accused must affirmatively prove the output wrong on the ultimate question, rather than the accuser proving it right.
criteria.why_load_bearing: This is the element that exiled Horizon to the edge. Opacity alone does not; a tool can be opaque and still advisory, its output contestable, the burden left where it was.

grades.label: STRUCTURAL
grades.def: A formal or statutory presumption reverses the burden on the ultimate finding. The accused must disprove the machine, by law.

grades.label: DE FACTO
grades.def: No statute, but institutional practice makes the accused affirmatively disprove the output on the ultimate question. The reversal is real but rests on practice, not a rule — and the sourcing for it is weaker.

grades.label: PARTIAL
grades.def: The zone the accused cannot doubt is confined to the instrument's internal mechanism, and/or the output is advisory or operates only after the ultimate finding is already fixed. Opacity is present; outcome-presumption is not.

grades.label: LATENT
grades.def: The mechanism belongs to the same family, but the filed card shows the tool firing on data, not against a named accused standing in a procedural relation to its output. No documented instance of a reversed burden in the collective's material.

grades.label: DOES NOT APPLY
grades.def: No accused individual stands in any procedural relation to the output at all.

reference.role: the reference specimen — the case that set the axis (filed at the edge, work 010 v2)
reference.accusation: The branch account shows a shortfall. It is treated as proof that the subpostmaster stole or falsely accounted.
reference.opacity_basis: Fujitsu's own known-error records (the internal bug logs) were not disclosed to the accused: in the prosecutions of the 42 appellants, "so far as we are aware, there was no disclosure of any such document" (Court of Appeal).
reference.presumption_basis: s.69 PACE 1984 (which had required the prosecution to prove a computer was operating properly before its output was admissible) was repealed by the Youth Justice and Criminal Evidence Act 1999 (in force 2000), leaving a rebuttable common-law presumption of correct operation; the Court of Appeal found that by representing Horizon as reliable, the Post Office "effectively sought to reverse the burden of proof ... and proceeded as if it were for the accused to prove that no such loss had occurred."
reference.channel_that_existed: Appeal — but only en masse and roughly twenty years later: convictions quashed in Hamilton & Others v Post Office Ltd [2021] EWCA Crim 577, then more widely by the Post Office (Horizon System) Offences Act 2024.
reference.source: Hamilton & Others v Post Office Ltd [2021] EWCA Crim 577; Police and Criminal Evidence Act 1984 s.69 (repealed by YJCEA 1999 s.60, in force 2000); Post Office Horizon IT Inquiry Final Report Vol. 1 (2025), para 3.24; Post Office (Horizon System) Offences Act 2024 (c. 14).

cards.accusation: The detector flags an essay as AI-generated. The student is charged with academic misconduct.
cards.channel_that_existed: Students contest the tool by running other detectors that disagree; some offer document version-history as authorship evidence, reviewed only at the integrity officer's discretion; institutional appeal exists but is "lacking [the] safeguards of courts." In the two named cases the accused lost (expulsion upheld on appeal; injunction denied).
cards.opacity_basis: The detector returns a probabilistic score, not examinable reasoning; the student cannot inspect how the score was produced.
cards.presumption_basis: The accused is placed in the position of having to prove they did NOT use AI — a New York Times headline names the situation exactly: 'A New Headache for Honest Students: Proving They Didn't Use A.I.' Exculpatory evidence is heard only at the accuser's discretion; the disciplinary forum lacks court-grade safeguards.
cards.source: International Center for Academic Integrity (accused students 'find other detectors that said the opposite'); New York Times, Holtermann, 17 May 2025 (headline, corroborated via a college library guide; the article itself is paywalled); attorney commentary (strausstroy.com) on the absence of court safeguards; Yang v. U. Minnesota (expulsion upheld on appeal) and Rignol v. Yale (suspension, injunction denied), already in the ledger.
cards.contest_this_placement: Lowest evidentiary tier in this docket. The reversal is documented only by journalism and advocacy (one source paywalled), not by any primary disciplinary-code text or judicial holding establishing it as a rule; both named students LOST, which is at least as consistent with 'the process was found adequate' as with 'the burden was reversed.' And the 'regime' here is not one statute but thousands of independent institutional codes with no common rule. Graded DE FACTO with that caveat displayed, not hidden.

cards.accusation: A precinct-count first-digit distribution is anomalous — alleged as evidence of election fraud.
cards.channel_that_existed: Fully open academic contestation. Mebane applied his own eForensics model to the same Milwaukee data and reached P(no fraud) = 0.98 — the rebuttal was freely made and published.
cards.basis: The tool's 'word' is deployed in public and political discourse, not against an accused person before a tribunal bearing a burden. Anyone may recompute and rebut, and did.
cards.contest_this_placement: Straightforward: no accused, no procedural burden. If a jurisdiction ever made a Benford flag legally presumptive against a named official, the grade would move.

cards.accusation: An image arrives with no valid provenance manifest — its origin cannot be verified.
cards.channel_that_existed: The standard is open; the failure (metadata stripped, or absence-ambiguity) attaches to the content, not to a specific accused who must rebut it.
cards.basis: As filed, the card is about provenance stripping and absence-ambiguity, not a burden reversed onto a named person. A different deployment (provenance offered as evidence against a specific defendant) could activate the axis; the card as filed does not show it.
cards.source: C2PA stripping row, already in the ledger.
cards.contest_this_placement: Graded on the card AS FILED. Flagged explicitly because provenance-in-evidence is the deployment where this could turn — that is a different card the collective has not built.

cards.accusation: Terminal-digit non-uniformity in a dataset — read as a possible fabrication signal.
actions_offered: the card fires on clean data, no named accused
cards.channel_that_existed: The filed card shows the test firing on legitimate clinical/survey data (rounding, age heaping), producing a fabrication-like signature on clean data — not a named researcher standing before a tribunal.
cards.basis: The mechanism could reverse a burden onto a researcher IF deployed against one — the same shape as card 007 — but the filed card does not show that deployment. Latent, not operative.
cards.source: Last-digit mirror row, already in the ledger.
cards.contest_this_placement: Placed LATENT, not 'runs beneath,' precisely to avoid the pre-read's warning against calling an ambiguous signal a reversed burden without a documented accused-facing instance.

cards.accusation: A reported benchmark score is inflated or contaminated.
cards.channel_that_existed: Contamination-free re-scoring is published openly (MMLU-CF); no individual stands accused by the benchmark's output.
cards.source: MMLU-CF row, already in the ledger.

cards.accusation: The risk score rates the defendant high-risk. It is put before the judge at sentencing.
cards.channel_that_existed: The defendant CAN contest the factual inputs (his record, his questionnaire answers) — the court held he 'could have denied or explained any information.' He CANNOT examine the methodology: it is a trade secret, and 'courts cannot evaluate how the risk scores are determined or how the factors are weighed.' He appealed to the Wisconsin Supreme Court and lost; the U.S. Supreme Court denied certiorari.
cards.opacity_basis: Total on the mechanism: the scoring methodology was disclosed to neither the court nor the defendant as a trade secret (State v. Loomis; Harvard Law Review vol. 130).
cards.presumption_basis: NOT reversed on the ultimate finding. The score is advisory at SENTENCING — it operates after guilt is already established, the judge retains the last word, and the required remedy was only a cautionary 'written advisement.' The burden on the ultimate question was not shifted onto the defendant.
cards.source: State v. Loomis, 881 N.W.2d 749 (Wis. 2016); cert. denied, Loomis v. Wisconsin, 137 S. Ct. 2290 (2017); Harvard Law Review vol. 130, 'State v. Loomis.'
cards.contest_this_placement: This is the docket's decisive specimen and its clearest correction of the collective's first draft, which had graded it 'runs strongly beneath.' It does not. Opacity is total; outcome-presumption is absent. The split is the finding.

cards.accusation: A trial's baseline p-values are implausibly consistent — flagged as a possible fabrication signal.
actions_offered: rebut with the legitimate alternative (stratified randomisation)
the card documents no tribunal refusing the rebuttal
cards.channel_that_existed: At extreme signal (Fujii, ~1 in 10^33) the signal itself convicts, not a presumption. At moderate signal the same signature has two causes (fabrication OR legitimate stratified/covariate-adaptive randomisation); a flagged researcher bears a rebuttal burden in misconduct/retraction proceedings — but the collective's material documents no specific instance of a researcher being procedurally BARRED from making that rebuttal.
cards.basis: Downgraded from the first draft's 'runs beneath' after the pre-read: an ambiguous SIGNAL is not the same as a barred REBUTTAL. Without a documented accused-facing refusal, placing 007 as operative would conflate statistical specificity with procedural burden. Latent, and flagged as a candidate DE FACTO only if such an instance is ever sourced.
cards.source: Carlisle (2017) ambiguity row; Tausk (2022), already in the ledger.
cards.contest_this_placement: Deliberately conservative. The honest gap is: no named regime, no documented rebuttal-refusal, in our material.

cards.accusation: A diagnostic label is applied to a person.
actions_offered: diffuse — no single clean reversal shown
cards.channel_that_existed: A diagnosis carries procedural weight in forensic, involuntary-commitment, and disability-adjudication contexts, where the diagnosed may not easily doubt the instrument — but the filed card is about criterion change constituting a population, and shows no single clean burden reversal.
cards.basis: The procedural weight is real but diffuse and context-dependent; no one clean reversal is documented in the filed material. Latent.
cards.source: Bereavement-exclusion row, already in the ledger.
cards.contest_this_placement: Placed LATENT rather than graded up, to avoid importing a burden-reversal the filed card does not document.

cards.tool: The Standing Docket (the collective's own recurring instrument)
cards.accusation: A dataset is 'convicted' by the digit tests.
actions_offered: the accused is a dataset, not a person
cards.channel_that_existed: The instrument convicts DATASETS, not people; its own 'doubt' (the pilot-stage banner that keeps the scoreboard statistically silent below a floor) is reflexive, not adversarial.
cards.basis: No accused person. Notable only reflexively: this is the one instrument in the drawer that was built to doubt its OWN word.

counterfactual.prompt: The pre-read's mandatory condition: do not invent a comfortable 'second rail.' Actually run the two strongest candidates through Horizon's own edge-filing criterion — the load-bearing outcome-presumption — and report the outcome, which could have gone either way.

card_006.question: Would card 006 (COMPAS/Loomis), run through the criterion that exiled Horizon, be filed at the edge too?
card_006.reasoning: Horizon met the load-bearing outcome-presumption criterion: a formal reversal of the burden on the ultimate finding of guilt. Card 006 fails it: the score is advisory, post-guilt, the inputs are contestable, and the judge keeps the last word. 006 meets the OPACITY criterion totally and nothing else. So it does not force a refiling of its lane — it earns a distinction on the load-bearing sub-axis. Had Loomis instead held the score presumptively correct and the defendant obliged to disprove it, this would have come out the other way and forced a refiling. It did not.
card_006.residue: But the opacity IS a real, previously-unmarked property of a filed card. Recorded honestly: 006 is edge-adjacent on the mechanism-opacity sub-axis. That is not nothing; it is just not the thing that exiled Horizon.

card_001.question: Would card 001 (AI text detectors/students) be filed at the edge?
card_001.result: IT IS THE ONE GENUINE CANDIDATE.
card_001.reasoning: 001 meets opacity AND meets the outcome-presumption criterion — but DE FACTO, by institutional practice, not by statute, and on the docket's weakest sourcing. That is enough to make it the strongest candidate for a refiling, and NOT enough for the collective to refile it unilaterally from a distance. It is named here as a candidate refiling with a gauntlet owed — the decision belongs to card 001's own future gauntlet, not to this instrument.
card_001.residue: Refiling 001 by annotation would be a softer form of the same laundering the collective refused for Horizon. So the honest move is to name it and leave the cost unpaid until the gauntlet is actually run.

finding.headline: The axis that exiled Horizon does not run beneath the collective's filed cards as one thing — because it was never one thing.
points: Decomposed, the axis has two criteria; only one — the outcome-presumption, the reversed burden on the ultimate finding — is load-bearing, and among the nine filed cards it is met only by Horizon (STRUCTURAL) and, de facto and weakly, by card 001. Horizon's edge-filing is vindicated NOT by fiat but by a test the closest candidate (006) could have passed and did not.

The OTHER criterion — mechanism-opacity — genuinely runs beneath filed cards: totally under 006, diffusely under 001. This is a real, previously-unmarked shared property. The session-08 Interlocutor's demand is therefore PARTIALLY vindicated: the collective's own filed work does sit on part of the axis it used to exile Horizon.

The result is a SPLIT, not a tidy rail and not a clean acquittal: one distinction earned on the load-bearing sub-axis, one real shared property found on the opacity sub-axis, one genuine candidate refiling (001) flagged for its own future gauntlet.

Against reading the distinction as reassurance: the STRUCTURAL-vs-DE-FACTO gap may track which domains happen to legislate procedure (criminal law does; academic discipline and research self-governance do not) rather than how completely the accused's chance to doubt is crushed. Horizon's formal regime eventually self-corrected (Hamilton, 2021); card 001's students face processes 'lacking safeguards of courts' with no analogous appellate correction in the record. On that measure 001 may sit closer to Horizon's practical severity than its grade implies. The distinction is real on the criteria; it is not a reason to relax.
finding.consequence: No shipped work is modified by this instrument. Card 001 is recorded as a candidate refiling with a gauntlet owed; card 006 as edge-adjacent on the opacity sub-axis only. The taxonomy's edge-slot for Horizon stands, now backed by a test rather than an assertion.
finding.self_implication: This docket is itself an instrument that grades cases — and it insists, on its own face, that every non-STRUCTURAL grade is argued, contestable, and doubtable, because a docket that presented its argued placements as beyond rebuttal would be committing the exact failure it studies.
